question: how can i check if my swing is straight?
content:
1. Film From Behind
Set the camera directly behind the bowler, lined up with:

The ball-side shoulder
The target line (the “invisible line” from the foul line to the breakpoint)
This angle shows the true path of the swing.

2. Draw the Invisible Line
The bowler imagines (or the coach draws on video):
A straight line from the gripping hand,
All the way to the breakpoint
This is the reference line the swing must stay aligned with.

3. Check Swing Direction
A professional straight swing has three checkpoints:

A) Pushaway
Should start slightly inside the shoulder line, not out to the right.
It must point toward the invisible line, not away from it.

B) Downswing / Backswing
The ball should travel beside the body, not behind the hip.
The swing plane should stay parallel to the invisible line.
No looping, no wrapping, no drifting behind the back.

C) Forward Swing
The ball returns on the same plane it went back.
It stays tight to the body, never crossing behind the spine.
The hand stays behind the ball, not rotating early.
If the swing stays on that plane from start to finish, it’s straight.

4. Match Swing to Target Line
The final check:
Does the swing stay aligned with the stance to the target and to the breakpoint line the entire time?

If yes:
The swing is straight
The release is clean
The ball will project exactly where the bowler intends

If not:
The bowler will miss inside or outside even with perfect timing.
